name: soaring-catalogue
title: A catalogue of soaring data — pointers, not payloads
description: Where to find the files a soaring flight computer needs. This package contains NO airspace, NO aerodromes and NO waypoints: it says where each file lives, who maintains it, under what terms, and what it does NOT contain. An airspace file goes wrong faster than a repository can be updated, and a stale copy is worse than none — so we point at the maintainers, who republish as it changes, and the app shows the pilot the date it downloaded.

licenses.name: AGPL-3.0
licenses.title: The catalogue itself (the pointers and their descriptions)

fields.name: uri
fields.title: Where the file actually lives
fields.description: We link. We do not rehost.

fields.name: source
fields.title: Who maintains it
fields.description: The people a correction should go to.

fields.name: licence
fields.title: Licence name
fields.description: EMPTY when we have not established it. Empty is a statement — not knowing is not the same as being allowed.

fields.name: redistributable
fields.title: May a cache also SHARE this file?
fields.description: FALSE when the licence is unknown. A cache that redistributes on a guess is a lawsuit with a pilot's name on it.

fields.name: updated
fields.title: Upstream's own last-update date
fields.description: EMPTY when upstream publishes none — an app must be able to say 'age unknown', because an airspace file is dangerous when stale and a blank date must never read as fresh.

fields.name: coverage
fields.title: What the file holds AND WHAT IT DOES NOT
fields.description: The second half is the one that prevents a pilot inferring a coverage nobody promised.
